commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Antigravity compact-icon resolution to prefer Gemini 5-hour and weekly quota-summary windows, uses provider style for merged-icon resolving, aligns rounded progress-bar fill/accessibility percent, and adds focused tests plus release-note text.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main ignores antigravity-quota-summary rows for compact icon lanes and passes combined style through the merged icon resolver. I did not run tests because this review is required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the patch is clean and proof is sufficient, leaving maintainer review and required-check completion as the next action.

target.title: Fix Antigravity Gemini icon lanes and exhausted bar fill
target.body: ## Summary - Show Antigravity Gemini session on the compact icon's top lane and Gemini weekly on the bottom lane. - Never borrow a missing Gemini cadence from Claude + GPT quota-summary rows. - Preserve current local/model-quota availability fallback when the quota-summary endpoint is unavailable. - Render fractional values that round to 0% or 100% as fully empty or full. ## Review and proof - `swift test list` passes after splitting an Intel Swift type-checker-heavy `#require` expression. - Focused icon, render-signature, and warning-marker suite: 51 tests passed again after the final current-main rebase. - Exact shard 0 command: all 120 groups passed locally. - `make check`: format and lint clean after the final current-main rebase. - Full branch autoreview against `origin/main`: clean, confidence 0.82. - CI failure diagnosed from the surfaced compiler output: Intel Swift could not type-check the compound bitmap lookup inside `#require`; current head moves the lookup outside the macro without changing behavior. - The temporary stacked CI-diagnostics commit was removed from this PR. Its equivalent landed separately in #1632 and is inherited only through `main`. Exact candidate: `6fd1bea0` ## Screenshots
target.author: zihao-qi
